title: devils-council Codex delegation scorecard (v1)
description: Strict schema enforced via `codex exec --output-schema` for Security / Dual-Deploy / Air-Gap persona deep-scan delegation. Mirrors skills/scorecard-schema/SKILL.md §Finding Fields — the six canonical required fields per finding (target, claim, evidence, ask, severity, category). `id` is stamped by the conductor (Phase 5 / CHAIR-06) post-merge; Codex is not asked to emit it. OpenAI strict structured-outputs compatibility: every property listed is required; additionalProperties is false at every level; minLength / format / pattern / default are OMITTED (rejected by OpenAI strict mode). Length/content enforcement is the conductor validator's job.

findings.description: Zero or more findings. Empty is valid (persona had no observations worth surfacing via deep scan).

target.description: Line reference (path:line or path:start-end) for code, heading anchor (## Heading) for plans/RFCs, or short quote-anchor phrase for prose.

claim.description: One-sentence statement of the specific failure mode. No generic 'consider edge cases' language (conductor validator enforces banned-phrase drop).

evidence.description: Verbatim substring of the reviewed artifact, minimum 8 characters enforced by the conductor validator (OpenAI strict mode rejects minLength).

ask.description: Concrete remediation the author can act on in a single line or short block.

severity.description: Canonical 4-tier severity enum. No `praise` tier; no `high`/`medium`/`low` (those are Codex-native and must be mapped by the conductor per scorecard-schema SKILL §Relationship to Codex Deep-Scan).

category.description: Free-text persona-suggested tag. Examples: complexity, observability, cost, business, auth-bypass, race-condition, iam-wildcard, air-gap, egress, blast-radius.
